FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Regional consolidation heats up with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while big banks face mixed signals on earnings upside and rate pressure.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaiian's return to mainland U.S. after BNP Paribas exit.
Q2 bank earnings poised to run hot on SpaceX IPO fees and geopolitical volatility trading, offsetting deposit-pricing pressure.
July rate hike odds rising on Strait of Hormuz oil spike—direct headwind to NIM compression and deposit dynamics.
CFPB nominee Brian Johnson wins 60-day discretionary review over staff cuts post-confirmation, signaling Trump influence on bureau workforce.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments sold to CalPERS for nonprofit conversion; RIA consolidation accelerates across platforms; Betterment faces cash sweep litigation.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments ($580B pension system gains multi-asset manager; Russell sheds debt, converts to non-profit governance mirroring Vanguard's playbook).
RIA consolidation hits overdrive: Hightower posts record organic growth, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Choreo closes fourth acquisition, Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Aspen-backed Summitry ($4B RIA) transitions founder Colin Higgins to chairman; President Katz and COO Wilkes elevated to co-CEOs after 22-year run.
Betterment hit with class action over cash sweep accounts—alleged sweeps into non-interest-bearing deposits generated hidden profits without client compensation.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: U.S. P&C insurers post best decade in underwriting profit, but geopolitical tensions and regional market gaps create divergent risks.

bullets.text: U.S. P&C industry hits strongest underwriting profit in 10 years with $84B in gains over two years and lowest combined ratio in a decade, driven by pricing and underwriting discipline across $1T in direct premiums.
Performance diverges sharply across lines despite industry strength—not all segments benefiting equally from the underwriting tailwind.
Hormuz Strait attacks on UAE tankers escalate geopolitical risk in a corridor handling 21% of global crude, likely spiking war-risk marine insurance premiums and P&L volatility.
Saudi Re takes 22.5% stake in Lloyd's operator AdA Risk for £8.95M, signaling deepening Middle East capital focus on London market underwriting.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Private debt stays resilient in North America while CMBS distress signals credit deterioration; fintech expansion and litigation risks emerge on the consumer side.

bullets.text: North America private debt remains LP-favored despite market noise, with AlbaCore noting clear divergence between performing and distressed credits—survival of the fit dynamics taking hold.
CMBS special servicing rate climbed in June 2026, led by large retail, office, and lodging distress—signals credit quality deterioration and likely servicer workouts ahead.
Nubank cleared for full banking in Mexico; Klarna hit with Dutch class action lawsuit—growth bets colliding with regulatory/litigation headwinds in key markets.
$40bn Swiss pension fund system underweighted private credit due to geographic/allocation mismatches but plans increased deployment despite near-term headwinds.

PE & Deals.lede: PE infrastructure and financing hitting new highs: Arctos breaks $6.2B with novel GP financing structure, while mega-cap energy plays and secondaries momentum accelerate.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closes $6.2B GP financing using 'self-liquidating' structure—a debut for this Keystone strategy in GP financing market, allowing capital returns to LPs while maintaining portfolio exposure.
Blackstone, Apollo, and KKR co-invest $5.34B for minority stakes in Williams Energy's Power Innovation projects, signaling major PE appetite for large-cap infrastructure.
Moelis hires Evercore VP as MD and pulls four-person team to build LP-led secondaries practice, capitalizing on expanding market with favorable pricing and liquidity conditions.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens with high bar to clear, but sector fundamentals look solid—though structural QT risks loom and capital markets are rotating beyond mega-cap tech.

bullets.text: Major bank earnings kick off Tuesday with market priced for perfection; any miss could trigger volatility after a strong run.
U.S. banks riding record NIMs, capital relief, and AI-driven cost cuts to near all-time high valuations—but QT poses underestimated structural risk if central banks tighten too aggressively.
Russell 2000 on best year since 2003 as money rotates out of mega-cap tech into small caps on AI capex and tax policy tailwinds.
ECB tapped 36 banks (including Deutsche, BPCE) to pilot digital euro in 2025, deliberately recruiting skeptics to pressure-test the model.

Newsletters.lede: Bank earnings dominate tomorrow with credit/deposit signals; meanwhile, tech and advisory workflows shift amid AI competition and policy changes.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—critical read on credit quality, deposit dynamics, and consumer health across lending and trading.
Alibaba's AI model now ranks first globally; cheaper Chinese alternatives are eroding US tech dominance in artificial intelligence, with ripple effects for US valuations.
Vanguard and Envestnet launched tools to minimize tax friction on model portfolio conversions—tackling a key RIA workflow friction point.
Student loan policy shifts are forcing advisors to rebuild college planning strategies, reshaping demand for education financing products.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 4 stories spanning incentive misalignment in sports, computational theory's role in competition, startup capital creativity in AI, and Apple's strategic lag.

bullets.text: NBA players gaming shooting percentages by taking low-value end-of-quarter heaves—textbook principal-agent problem where individual metrics destroy team value.
Tyler Cowen: Markets stay competitive only if P ≠ NP; if P = NP, firms can efficiently detect and sustain cartels, collapsing competition.
Groq's Jonathan Ross used unconventional financing (Grok Bonds) to land NVIDIA partnership after near-bankruptcy—demonstrates creative capital solutions in AI infrastructure deals.
Apple's OpenAI lawsuit over trade-secret theft signals a bigger problem: no coherent AI strategy and playing catchup to tech rivals.

all.lede: Regional bank M&A heating up as First Hawaiian targets TriCo; big bank earnings week begins tomorrow amid QT headwinds.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring California's TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks First Hawaiian's mainland U.S. re-entry after BNP Paribas exit.
Five of six largest U.S. banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow; results will signal credit quality, deposit stability, and consumer health across key business lines.
QE structurally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making quantitative tightening riskier than assumed because banks now depend on central bank liquidity support.
